description:
Trigger: an application uses two PKCS#11 tokens with different PINs, e.g. a code-signing token and a TLS-key token. Both Pkcs11Backend instances read the same process-global PKCS11_PIN env var.

Maintenance consequence: this is an unsolvable footgun — there is no way for the user to provide a different PIN per backend instance without (a) racing set_var/from_uri/remove_var across threads, or (b) sharing the same PIN across both tokens. The user will file an issue; the answer is 'don't do that' or 'we don't support it'.

Fix: support per-instance PIN via either (a) ?pin= URI param (with security warning), (b) a builder API Pkcs11Backend::builder().lib(...).slot(...).label(...).pin(...).build(), or (c) per-URI env var name like ?pin_env=MY_TOKEN_PIN that the from_uri resolves. Option (b) is most idiomatic and gives the caller programmatic control over PIN handling without putting it in URIs that may get logged. Document PKCS11_PIN as a 'simple deployment fallback'.
title: Process-global PKCS11_PIN env var is read once at construction; doesn't compose with multi-token apps
